parityMeta.describes: iOS-17e-6a — fineTune LIVE, legacy-respect HONORED. A compound templateExercise (repMin 6 / repMax 8 / sets 3 / progressionUnitKg 2.5 / progressionPercent [5,10]) with three RECENT in-window push sessions at a FLAT top weight (60 kg, all 3x8 = hit ceiling) drives shouldAdd. With asOfDate = deterministicClockIso, the LIVE buildSetWeightFineTune sees a flat e1RM trend (slope 0) -> projects exactly lastWeight 60: applyFineTuneIfDataRich clamps (±10% [54,66]), 2.5-rounds (60), then the legacy-respect guard FIRES (baseline 62.5 > lastWeight 60 AND rounded 60 <= lastWeight 60) -> returns the legacy baseline 62.5 (the user hit the top of the range every set, that IS ready-to-add even with a flat regression). Output equals makeSuggestion(nil), but the golden's data-rich fineTuneNeutrality (samplesUsed 9, fallbackReason null) PROVES the live fineTune RAN and the legacy-respect branch chose baseline over the flat projection. FUNCTION-LEVEL compute-assert on the Swift side.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: asOfDate=true -> generator passes deterministicClockIso as the injected fineTune clock. RECENT in-window history (daysAgo 4/11/18) all 60kg @8 -> flat e1RM 76 (slope 0) -> projected weightForTarget(8) 60 -> rounded 60 <= lastWeight 60 with a legacy +increment(2.5)=62.5 wanting to add -> legacy-respect returns 62.5. Sessions NEWEST-FIRST.
